codeReview.summary: Well-structured ablation study implementation covering all 4 required ablation dimensions with per-category breakdowns, formatted Markdown tables, and machine-readable JSON output. The script reads from existing benchmark data (final-suite, competitor-baselines, M1 embedding spikes) and produces comprehensive analysis. All 5 expected behaviors are met: (1) hybrid vs semantic-only with per-category metrics, (2) hybrid vs lexical-only with per-category metrics, (3) reranker on/off with both quality and latency, (4) 3 embedding model comparison with quality and latency. The commit includes 3 files: an 821-line Python script (exempt from size budgets per AGENTS.md), a 434-line Markdown report, and a 485-line JSON results file.

issues.description: Multiple nDCG@10 values exceed 1.0 (e.g., intent/semantic_only=1.468, config/hybrid=1.227, intent/bge-en-icl=1.013). Standard nDCG is bounded [0,1]. These values come from upstream evaluation data, not from this script — the ablation script faithfully passes through source metrics without range validation. The root cause is likely in the eval harness nDCG computation (possibly a weighted/non-normalized variant). This undermines metric credibility but is an upstream issue.

Ablation 2 hardcoded analysis text says '+557% MRR' for intent search, but the actual data-driven delta is ~+589% (BM25-only MRR=0.067, Hybrid MRR=0.460). The analysis text blocks are hardcoded rather than computed from the data, causing minor inconsistencies with the data-driven tables above them. Tables are correct.
Dead code: `for m in available: pass  # Will build rows below` — this loop does nothing and should be removed.
Missing trailing newline at end of JSON file (shown by git diff: '\ No newline at end of file'). Minor POSIX compliance issue.

Ablation 1 (hybrid vs semantic-only) compares data from different task sets: 17 tasks (hybrid/final benchmarks) vs 21 tasks (semantic-only/M1 baseline). The report acknowledges this in the Data Sources table and partially in the intent search analysis, but per-category comparisons may be misleading since different tasks are included. Ablations 2-3 use matched task sets (17 tasks each) and don't have this issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Benchmark-worker skill Step 4 says 'Run each timing measurement at least 3 times' but this ablation study analyzed existing pre-computed data rather than running live benchmarks. The skill could add guidance for data-analysis-focused benchmark tasks that don't involve live tool execution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The benchmark-worker skill Step 4 mentions running timing measurements 3+ times, but for ablation studies that analyze existing data (no new live runs), this doesn't apply. Consider adding guidance for data-analysis-focused benchmark tasks.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: nDCG values exceeding 1.0 in the eval harness output suggest a non-standard nDCG computation variant. This affects all downstream benchmark reports and ablation studies. The architecture library documents M2 benchmark results with nDCG but doesn't note this known anomaly or explain which nDCG variant is used.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ablation_results.json contains nDCG values of 1.468 (intent/semantic_only), 1.227 (config/hybrid), 1.013 (intent/bge-en-icl). These propagate from upstream eval harness data in benchmarks/results/final-suite/ and spikes/embedding-chunking/results/.

summary: PASS. The ablation-studies feature delivers all 4 required ablation studies with comprehensive per-category breakdowns, formatted Markdown tables, and machine-readable JSON output. The Python script is well-structured, loading data from 3 existing data sources and producing a thorough 434-line report. All 5 expected behaviors from the feature spec are met. Five non-blocking issues were found: (1) nDCG>1.0 values from upstream eval harness, (2) minor hardcoded analysis text inconsistency with computed tables, (3) a dead code loop, (4) missing trailing newline in JSON, (5) mismatched task-set sizes in ablation 1. None of these block acceptance — the core deliverables are correct and complete.
